definition: A type of amplitude used in quantum field theory, particularly in the context of graviton interactions.
examples: Used in calculations of graviton scattering processes.
Extended to analyze nonzero graviton tree amplitudes.

definition: A framework developed to assess the impact of AI on educational outcomes over time.

definition: An environment that provides persistent orchestration and memory for multi-step AI workflows.

Facilitates secure execution of AI tasks.

definition: A benchmark designed to evaluate the efficiency of AI coding agents in accelerating federal permitting processes.
examples: Demonstrates potential to reduce NEPA drafting time.

definition: A platform developed by OpenAI for building and deploying AI applications at scale.
examples: Integrated with AWS to enhance AI capabilities.
Supports custom models and enterprise AI agents.

definition: A model in the Gemini series optimized for speed and cost-efficiency in AI tasks.

definition: An advanced image generation model that combines high-speed processing with extensive world knowledge.
